description_long: This object consists of a stack of four wooden Euro pallets, with one additional pallet leaning against the stack at an angle. The pallets are constructed from rough, weathered wooden planks, each featuring three horizontal beams supporting several vertical slats on the top and bottom surfaces. The sides of the pallets are stamped with 'EPAL' and 'EUR' markings, indicating their compliance with European Pallet Association standards. The wood shows signs of wear, discoloration, and some darkened areas, suggesting extensive use and exposure to the elements. The open slat design allows for airflow and easy handling by forklifts or pallet jacks. The overall structure is robust, with visible gaps between the slats and a utilitarian, industrial appearance.
description: A stack of four weathered wooden Euro pallets with one pallet leaning against the stack, featuring open slats and stamped EPAL/EUR markings.
description_view_0: Side view showing the stack of four pallets with one pallet leaning diagonally against the stack, clearly displaying the EPAL/EUR stamps.
description_view_1: End-on view of the stacked pallets, highlighting the open slat design and the layered construction of the wooden beams.
description_view_2: Opposite side view similar to view 0, showing the stacked pallets and the angled pallet, with visible wood grain and stamps.
description_view_3: Top-down view revealing the arrangement of the slats and the rectangular shape of the pallet stack.
